content.claim: Hungary has transformed former special institutions into unified special educational/conductive-pedagogical resource centres (EGYMI) since the early 2000s, providing both special education and integrative services to support mainstream teacher capacity for inclusive practice; this non-structural support infrastructure predates the current identification rise and was designed for integration rather than segregation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: UN rapporteur (#4) asserts infrastructure inadequacy but no independent quantification of EGYMI capacity relative to current caseload provided
Sufficiency of EGYMI network to support identified caseload growth remains unverified
Resource allocation and staffing levels of EGYMI centres not specified in available sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
